jokes.text: TV parody of procedural shows: 'Some cops can read minds. Did you do it or not? I'm not a mind reader.'
jokes.setup: Parody of TV cop show tropes - cop with mind reading powers
jokes.punchline: Cop immediately says he can't read minds
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of procedural tropes with perfect comedic timing - the contradiction is immediate and absurd

jokes.text: 'I could solve this crime if only I had personally watched it happen.'
jokes.setup: Cop who can 'see the past' as a special ability
jokes.punchline: Reveals the ability is just literally watching things happen
jokes.explanation: Continues the parody with logical absurdity - 'seeing the past' is just being a witness

jokes.text: Angel Gabriel refusing to help cop: 'Nah, man, I ain't seen nothing for no cop.'
jokes.setup: Cop gets help from angels in procedural parody
jokes.punchline: Angel Gabriel speaks like a street-wise witness who won't snitch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character subversion - the Archangel Gabriel as an uncooperative street witness is both shocking and perfectly executed

jokes.text: 'There's only one way to know who committed this crime. I'll just have to... find out.'
jokes.setup: Cop with no special abilities must solve crime
jokes.punchline: His method is literally just 'find out' - basic police work presented as revelation
jokes.explanation: Solid anti-climax joke - builds up to basic detective work as if it's profound

jokes.text: Lrrr watching TV: 'This truly is the golden age of television.'
jokes.setup: After watching terrible parody procedural shows
jokes.punchline: Alien ruler earnestly praising obviously bad television
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lrrr's earnest appreciation of terrible TV reflects his alien perspective

jokes.text: 'Right now, NBC needs my love more than you do.'
jokes.setup: Son asking father for help with merit badge
jokes.punchline: Alien ruler prioritizes television network over his child
jokes.explanation: Strong character comedy - absurd prioritization that's both alien and relatable to TV addiction

jokes.text: 'Either help the boy, or go outside and degauss the lawn!'
jokes.setup: Typical domestic argument between parents
jokes.punchline: Alien chore 'degauss the lawn' instead of normal yard work
jokes.explanation: Excellent sci-fi comedy - 'degaussing' (magnetic field removal) applied to lawn care is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: 'Well, let's work up to that one.'
jokes.setup: Just mentioned patricide merit badge
jokes.punchline: Treats killing his father as just needing practice first
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up to patricide joke - casual parental planning of his own murder

jokes.text: 'That is for Grrrl Scouts! Men bring the skulls home, women arrange them.'
jokes.setup: Skull Arranging merit badge suggestion
jokes.punchline: Alien gender roles where skull arrangement is women's work
jokes.explanation: Good alien culture joke - takes traditional gender roles and applies them to skull-based activities

jokes.text: 'Good news, everyone! I'm in terrible pain.'
jokes.setup: Professor's signature 'Good news, everyone!' opening
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts by saying it's not good news
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth subversion of his own catchphrase - efficient and character-perfect

jokes.text: 'my arthritis will make me seize up like a dried monkey carcass.'
jokes.setup: Professor describing his arthritis pain
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison to dried monkey carcass
jokes.explanation: Typical Professor over-specific scientific analogy that's both disgusting and oddly precise

jokes.text: 'I've seen those. In the Great Monkey Desert.'
jokes.setup: Professor mentions dried monkey carcasses
jokes.punchline: Fry casually confirms he's seen them, with made-up location
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment - his casual confirmation of absurd things fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: 'We interrupt your TV being off for this special bulletin.'
jokes.setup: Standard news bulletin interruption
jokes.punchline: Interrupting 'TV being off' instead of a show
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of news bulletin cliché - treating silence as a program being interrupted

jokes.text: Jrrr's nervous invasion attempt: 'Um, hi? I-I'm Jrrr, of the planet Omicron Persei 8. I just wanted to know-- well, I'm here to invade your planet?'
jokes.setup: Child attempting first planetary invasion
jokes.punchline: Polite, uncertain delivery of invasion threat with question inflection
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - child's nervousness makes invasion attempt adorably ineffective

jokes.text: I just wanted to know-- well, I'm here to invade your planet?
jokes.setup: Jrrr tries to sound intimidating
jokes.punchline: His invasion threat comes out as an uncertain question
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jrrr's lack of confidence. The upward inflection makes it pathetic.

jokes.text: Nixon and Agnew laughing at cute invasion before gun powers up and 'Uh-oh.'
jokes.setup: Political figures find child invader amusing
jokes.punchline: Sudden threat escalation with weapon activation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy - cute moment immediately becomes threatening

jokes.text: 'before I get my expletives deleted!'
jokes.setup: Lrrr threatening Earth leaders
jokes.punchline: References TV censorship as if it's a physical threat
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke treating broadcast censorship as a tangible consequence

jokes.text: 'His bowels aren't gonna disem themselves.'
jokes.setup: Lrrr pressuring Jrrr to be more violent
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to disembowelment as routine activity
jokes.explanation: Dark humor fitting Lrrr's character - treating gruesome violence as mundane parenting advice

jokes.text: 'Just pick something and blow it up so I can get home in time for The Finder-Outer.'
jokes.setup: Father pressuring son to commit violence during invasion
jokes.punchline: Motivation is getting home to watch TV show with generic detective name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lrrr's priorities - conquest is secondary to television consumption

jokes.text: 'He killed my second-to-last Agnew.'
jokes.setup: Jrrr accidentally kills Agnew during failed invasion
jokes.punchline: Nixon has multiple Agnew clones and this is only the second-to-last
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - implies Nixon has been through multiple Agnew clones with casual precision

jokes.text: 'Clone of Agnew is gone, killed in his clomping shoes by ruthless Omicronians.'
jokes.setup: Nixon's presidential address about Agnew's death
jokes.punchline: 'Clomping shoes' as significant detail in tragedy, plus 'clone' casualty
jokes.explanation: Good attention to absurd detail - 'clomping shoes' as memorial detail is oddly specific

jokes.text: Aw, crap, he's on every channel!
jokes.setup: Nixon begins his planetary address
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's annoyed reaction to having his TV viewing interrupted
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction showing Lrrr's TV addiction trumping major political events.

jokes.text: 'Where's my Hot in Cleveland?' during embargo announcement
jokes.setup: Nixon announcing trade embargo affects cultural exports
jokes.punchline: Lrrr specifically worried about low-quality sitcom
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate panic about losing access to mediocre Earth television

jokes.text: 'If we want to breathe sulfuric acid and get our heads torn off by giant lizards, we can always go to Gary, Indiana.'
jokes.setup: Describing dangerous conditions on Omicron Persei 8
jokes.punchline: Gary, Indiana presented as equally horrible alternative
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor - comparing alien planet dangers to Indiana city is unexpected and effective

jokes.text: 'Even I would never send you to Gary, Indiana.'
jokes.setup: Leela's Gary, Indiana comparison
jokes.punchline: Professor confirms it's worse than alien death planet
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Gary, Indiana joke - Professor's confirmation makes it funnier

jokes.text: 'Sweet toke of Ocracoke, this stuff's the Omichronic!'
jokes.setup: Bender examining the Sacred Weed
jokes.punchline: Made-up stoner exclamation combining location and 'chronic' slang
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay perfectly suited to Bender's character - combines marijuana slang with alien planet name

jokes.text: 'Then engage deflector shields.' 'That's from TV, Geordi.'
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting sci-fi technology solutions
jokes.punchline: Bender points out she's quoting Star Trek
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor acknowledging sci-fi TV tropes within a sci-fi show - self-aware but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: 'Well, what sort of stealth technology do we have? A can of paint.'
jokes.setup: Asking about sophisticated stealth technology
jokes.punchline: Ship's stealth capability is literal paint can
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of sci-fi expectations - advanced stealth reduced to basic paint

jokes.text: 'Home, eh? That's where I live. Let them through the blockade.'
jokes.setup: Omicronian ship requesting passage home
jokes.punchline: Officer's redundant observation about what 'home' means
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy - military officer's unnecessary clarification is endearingly dim

jokes.text: 'Screwed again, my friend.' (Zoidberg's reaction to being left out of buddy system)
jokes.setup: Everyone paired up for dangerous mission except Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's resigned acceptance of being excluded
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg moment - his perpetual victimization delivered with characteristic pathos

jokes.text: 'I think it's leaking enchantment.' 'That's not enchantment, it's the herbs!'
jokes.setup: Fry notices Bender glowing mystically
jokes.punchline: Scientific explanation for magical appearance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from fantasy to science fiction explanation for glowing effect

jokes.text: 'Everyone, start filling these dime bags.'
jokes.setup: Need to collect scientific samples of herbs
jokes.punchline: Professor casually provides drug dealing equipment for science
jokes.explanation: Subversive humor - legitimate science using drug dealing paraphernalia, perfectly Professor

jokes.text: 'First Law of Robotics, baby.'
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Bender will warn him of danger
jokes.punchline: Bender references Asimov's laws while being completely untrustworthy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - referencing robot protection laws while planning to abandon Fry

jokes.text: 'I saw him in the, uh, kitchen. With Dinah. You know, the dyno-matic food-service robot.'
jokes.setup: Bender lying about Fry's whereabouts after abandoning him
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate lie with fake robot girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good Bender lie - characteristic over-elaboration when simple dishonesty would work

jokes.text: Omicronian TV show with canned laughter and grunting
jokes.setup: Lrrr complaining local shows are unwatchable
jokes.punchline: Alien sitcom is literally just grunting with laugh track
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about alien entertainment being incomprehensible to humans (and vice versa)

jokes.text: Fry eating Jrrr's feces thinking it's candy
jokes.setup: Jrrr offers Fry 'candy' which is actually his waste
jokes.punchline: Fry enjoys eating alien excrement unknowingly
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's somewhat cheap but fits the show's willingness to be disgusting

jokes.text: You're an alien? Do you speak English? English?
jokes.setup: Fry and Jrrr meet for first time
jokes.punchline: Fry asks if he speaks English while they're speaking English
jokes.explanation: Obvious irony joke. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: 'Feces pieces? Blech! You must be hungry. Really hungry.'
jokes.setup: Fry realizes he ate alien feces
jokes.punchline: Concludes he must be extremely hungry rather than being disgusted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - rationalizes eating feces as hunger rather than poor judgment

jokes.text: but I'm kind of picky.
jokes.setup: Jrrr offers to feed the hungry Fry after he ate alien poop
jokes.punchline: Fry claims to be picky after literally eating shit
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic understatement. After eating alien feces, claiming to be picky is absurd.

jokes.text: No, 'turd-eater.'
jokes.setup: Fry asks if 'T' stands for terrestrial
jokes.punchline: Jrrr matter-of-factly explains it's for eating his poop
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. The casualness of the nickname explanation is funny.

jokes.text: 'I'm gonna call you 'T.' For terrestrial?' 'No, turd-eater.'
jokes.setup: Jrrr giving Fry a nickname, Fry assumes scientific term
jokes.punchline: Actually referencing the feces-eating incident
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and callback - subverts expectation of dignified nickname

jokes.text: '(mocking) 'cause he can't watch television.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why his father is angry at humans
jokes.punchline: Child mocking father's trivial reason for interplanetary hatred
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing child's perspective on adult priorities

jokes.text: Both characters simultaneously saying Lrrr's full title: 'Ruler of the planet Omicron Persei 8.'
jokes.setup: Fry recognizes Jrrr's father
jokes.punchline: Both recite pompous title in unison
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to running gag - the formal title is so well-known both characters know it

jokes.text: He went out to buy you flowers. Whatever kind you like best. 'Cause he loves you and crap.
jokes.setup: Leela asks where Fry is
jokes.punchline: Bender's overly elaborate lie with crude ending
jokes.explanation: Good Bender lying - starts romantic then undercuts it with 'and crap.' Typical of his character.

jokes.text: Fry is one guy I'd definitely never abandon.
jokes.setup: Professor praises Fry's thoughtfulness
jokes.punchline: Bender's false claim while literally having abandoned Fry
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony. Bender's declaration is the exact opposite of what he just did.

jokes.text: Jrrr showing Fry weapons: 'This is my fleam. You use it to peel the skin off your enemies layer by layer till they die or whatever.'
jokes.setup: Child showing off his possessions to new friend
jokes.punchline: Casual description of torture device ending with dismissive 'or whatever'
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - child's casual attitude toward gruesome torture device, perfect alien culture clash

jokes.text: My friend Bender, who I'll never see again.
jokes.setup: Fry talks about the fleaming tool
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of being abandoned forever
jokes.explanation: Good pathos mixed with humor. Fry's matter-of-fact acceptance of abandonment is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Bender talking to Fry's bed: 'I didn't abandon you! You wandered off! Stop judging me, Fry-bed!'
jokes.setup: Bender alone in apartment feeling guilty
jokes.punchline: Having argument with inanimate object, projecting guilt
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bender's guilt manifesting as arguments with furniture

jokes.text: 'Oh, it's just not the same without the muffled screams.'
jokes.setup: Bender missing something about Fry's bed
jokes.punchline: Implies he enjoyed Fry's sleeping distress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender sadism - missing his friend but for characteristically selfish reasons

jokes.text: Fry's voicemail: 'we're out getting drunk at the Olive Garden'
jokes.setup: Standard party announcement in voicemail
jokes.punchline: Olive Garden as unlikely party destination
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor - family restaurant chain as party location fits Fry's character

jokes.text: Bender manipulating voicemail: 'I'm... drunk... right now. So just leave. I love you. Olive... you.'
jokes.setup: Bender editing Fry's voicemail to fool Leela
jokes.punchline: 'I love you' becomes 'Olive you' through editing, maintaining restaurant reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay and callback - Bender's technological manipulation creates accidental romance

jokes.text: Bender's voicemail manipulation: 'Mice... Mice... Myself... Elf... Elf.' 'Elves are back.'
jokes.setup: Bender trying to edit 'exterminating mice' message
jokes.punchline: Professor accepts 'elves are back' as normal workplace problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - Professor's casual acceptance of elf infestation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: 'Watch out! Sorry. This planet has a moon problem.'
jokes.setup: Jrrr and Fry nearly hit by falling moon debris
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation of planet having 'moon problem'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement - treating cosmic catastrophe as minor inconvenience with perfect economy

jokes.text: 'It's powered by love.' 'Aww.' 'Okay, stop loving me so much so we can land.'
jokes.setup: Romantic explanation for vehicle power source
jokes.punchline: Too much affection becomes practical flight problem
jokes.explanation: Creative sci-fi comedy - love as literal fuel creates practical problems for friendship

jokes.text: Those are the letters that Fry knows!
jokes.setup: Bender sees S.O.S. signal
jokes.punchline: Identifies it as within Fry's limited literacy
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor playing on Fry's supposed stupidity and Bender's knowledge of his limitations.

jokes.text: 'Jrrr, how do I watch a funny on the YouTube?'
jokes.setup: Powerful alien ruler needing help
jokes.punchline: Asking child how to use YouTube with awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Relatable generational humor - powerful alien struggling with technology like any parent

jokes.text: 'I thought I told you to kill those foos.'
jokes.setup: Lrrr finding Jrrr playing foosball
jokes.punchline: Treats foosball players as actual enemies to be eliminated
jokes.explanation: Excellent alien logic - taking game literally as military campaign fits Lrrr's character perfectly

jokes.text: 'You shot your hamster.' 'I told you, that was suicide.'
jokes.setup: Lrrr citing precedent for Jrrr killing pets
jokes.punchline: Child's casual correction about pet's cause of death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - child casually correcting father about pet hamster suicide with perfect timing

jokes.text: 'The week I spent hiding in your closet, eating your turds, was the best week of my life.'
jokes.setup: Emotional farewell between friends
jokes.punchline: Heartfelt declaration includes disgusting detail about eating feces
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - genuine emotion undercut by gross callback, very Fry-like sentiment

jokes.text: 'He's escaping on a bicycle powered by love. Which is an ordinary thing on this planet.'
jokes.setup: Dramatic chase scene with fantastical vehicle
jokes.punchline: Guard's matter-of-fact explanation deflates the drama
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of dramatic moment through mundane explanation - alien normalcy

jokes.text: 'Love harder!' 'We can't, sir. The men have been hurt too often.'
jokes.setup: Military commander demanding more speed in chase
jokes.punchline: Soldiers emotionally unable to love due to battlefield trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - military PTSD preventing emotional vehicle operation

jokes.text: 'I could, but he'd only live another 80 years at most. The humane thing is to put him to sleep.'
jokes.setup: Alien doctor evaluating human patient's condition
jokes.punchline: 80 years considered inhumanely short lifespan worth euthanasia
jokes.explanation: Excellent perspective humor - alien doctor's idea of mercy based on species longevity differences

jokes.text: 'It's not lame, it's lamé.'
jokes.setup: Jrrr calling his father's cape lame
jokes.punchline: Lrrr corrects pronunciation to French fabric term
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay - Lrrr's vanity about cape material shows character priorities

jokes.text: which is delicious, by the way; I've been drinking it every day--
jokes.setup: Jrrr describes Fry as full of red juice
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to regularly drinking Fry's blood
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor delivered casually. The revelation that he's been drinking Fry's blood is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: 'which is delicious, by the way; I've been drinking it every day'
jokes.setup: Lrrr describing human as skin ball full of red juice
jokes.punchline: Casual admission he's been drinking Fry's blood daily
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Lrrr's casual cannibalistic habit mentioned mid-speech

jokes.text: 'Good news! Nobody has to kill him after all. Because he already died!'
jokes.setup: Doctor interrupting family argument about mercy killing
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic announcement that patient died naturally
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy with good timing - doctor's cheerful death announcement during family conflict

jokes.text: 'At least his butt is.' (Fry's glowing posterior indicating he's alive)
jokes.setup: Everyone mourning Fry's apparent death
jokes.punchline: Glowing buttocks as sign of life
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback - scientific explanation for glowing creates absurd resurrection indicator

jokes.text: 'The electromagnetic intensity of the robot's friendship is causing the herbs in his bowels to bioluminesce with love. Probably.'
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation for Fry's glowing resurrection
jokes.punchline: Overly technical description ending with uncertain 'Probably'
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor pseudo-science - elaborate technical explanation undermined by uncertainty

jokes.text: 'Stuck on another delivery that he undertook on his own.'
jokes.setup: Fry starting to explain his alien adventure
jokes.punchline: Bender quickly covers with more lies
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender damage control - maintains deception with quick thinking

jokes.text: 'Remember that make out session in that dark closet? You were like a machine.'
jokes.setup: Leela referencing romantic moment with Fry
jokes.punchline: Unknown to her, she was making out with Bender the robot
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Leela's compliment about machine-like performance unknowingly accurate
